context: When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server. Otherwise, queries will not be allowed in the standby server.
The default value is three connections. The value must be less than max_connections. This parameter can only be set at server start.

Larger settings for shared_buffers usually require a corresponding increase in max_wal_size, in order to spread out the process of writing large quantities of new or changed data over a longer period of time.
If you are using prepared transactions, you will probably want max_prepared_transactions to be at least as large as max_connections, so that every session can have a prepared transaction pending.
When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server. Otherwise, queries will not be allowed in the standby server.
The shared lock table tracks locks on max_locks_per_transaction * (max_connections + max_prepared_transactions) objects.
The shared predicate lock table tracks locks on max_pred_locks_per_transaction * (max_connections + max_prepared_transactions) objects.
The memory limit for a hash table is computed by multiplying work_mem by hash_mem_multiplier.
It defaults to -1, indicating that the value of maintenance_work_mem should be used instead.
The shared lock table tracks locks on max_locks_per_transaction * (max_connections + max_prepared_transactions) objects.
The shared predicate lock table tracks locks on max_pred_locks_per_transaction * (max_connections + max_prepared_transactions) objects.
The number of buffers written per round (bgwriter_lru_maxpages) is applied after each bgwriter_delay sleep.
When running a standby server, you must set this parameter to the same or higher value than on the master server. Otherwise, queries will not be allowed in the standby server.
When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers, max_parallel_maintenance_workers, and max_parallel_workers_per_gather.
When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers, max_parallel_maintenance_workers, and max_parallel_workers_per_gather.
When changing this value, consider also adjusting max_parallel_workers, max_parallel_maintenance_workers, and max_parallel_workers_per_gather.
The number of workers per gather node is limited by max_parallel_workers; larger values have no effect.
Since parallel workers are taken from worker process pool, max_worker_processes also limits this value.
The number of parallel workers used for maintenance is limited by max_parallel_workers.
Also, note that a setting for this value which is higher than max_worker_processes will have no effect, since parallel workers are taken from the pool of worker processes established by that setting.
The default setting of -1 selects a size equal to 1/32nd (about 3%) of shared_buffers, but not less than 64kB nor more than the size of one WAL segment, typically 16MB.

It is ignored unless archive_mode was enabled at server start. If archive_command is an empty string (the default) while archive_mode is enabled, WAL archiving is temporarily disabled, but the server continues to accumulate WAL segment files in the expectation that a command will soon be provided.
To limit how old unarchived data can be, you can set archive_timeout to force the server to switch to a new WAL segment file periodically.
Also, wal_level must be set to replica or higher to allow connections from standby servers.
Also, wal_level must be set to replica or higher to allow replication slots to be used.
synchronous_standby_names has no effect unless synchronous_commit is set to on, remote_apply, or remote_write.
Logical replication workers are taken from the pool defined by max_worker_processes.
The synchronization workers are taken from the pool defined by max_logical_replication_workers.
Reducing this value relative to seq_page_cost will cause the system to prefer index scans; raising it will make index scans look relatively more expensive.
Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner, resulting in non-optimal plans.
Setting this value to geqo_threshold or more may trigger use of the GEQO planner, resulting in non-optimal plans.
This is on by default; however, track_counts must also be enabled for autovacuum to work.
If -1 is specified, the regular vacuum_cost_delay value will be used.
If -1 is specified (which is the default), the regular vacuum_cost_limit value will be used.

VACUUM will silently limit the effective value to 95% of autovacuum_freeze_max_age, so that a periodic manual VACUUM has a chance to run before an anti-wraparound autovacuum is launched for the table.

relation: less than or equal to half
context: VACUUM will silently limit the effective value to half the value of autovacuum_freeze_max_age, so that there is not an unreasonably short time between forced autovacuums.
VACUUM will silently limit the effective value to 95% of autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age, so that a periodic manual VACUUM has a chance to run before an anti-wraparound is launched for the table.
